novel_features.name: Cross-org health audit
novel_features.description: Find stale targets, orphaned resources, and missing role bindings across every org you administer, in one command.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining resources, targets, and roles across all orgs in the local SQLite store; no single Pangolin endpoint returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks 'is anything broken in my Pangolin stack?', this is the single command that answers it — no dashboard tab-clicking.

novel_features.name: Certificate expiry watch
novel_features.description: List certificates sorted by days-until-expiry across every org, with a configurable warning window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fetching certs per org+domain and joining with local-store metadata to sort by expiry — Pangolin's UI shows certs only per-resource.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks 'what certs expire soon?', skip the dashboard and get an actionable list with one call.

novel_features.name: Full config backup
novel_features.description: Export the complete Pangolin configuration — orgs, sites, resources, targets, roles, IdPs — as version-controllable JSON.
novel_features.rationale: Walks all 14 entity types and emits a normalized tree the user can diff in git; no Pangolin endpoint returns a full-config snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user wants a disaster-recovery snapshot or a config diff between dates, this is the artifact.

novel_features.name: Config restore from backup
novel_features.description: Re-apply a backup against a fresh Pangolin install, in the correct dependency order, with dry-run preview.
novel_features.rationale: Sequences mutations (org first, then site, resource, target, role bindings) with rollback-on-error — impossible without local dependency graph.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user rebuilds a Pangolin host or moves between machines, this is the way back up.

novel_features.name: Access graph
novel_features.description: Answer 'who can reach what' by joining users, roles, resources, and orgs into one queryable view.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local-store join across four entity types; the API exposes each piece separately but no endpoint returns the joined access view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user asks 'what does $person have access to?' or 'who can hit this resource?', this is the answer.

novel_features.name: One-shot expose
novel_features.description: Create site (if needed), create resource, attach target, bind a role — in one command, with dry-run preview.
novel_features.rationale: Sequences 3-5 API calls atomically with rollback-on-error; replaces a 5-tab dashboard dance with a single scriptable invocation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user (or an agent) wants to expose a homelab service, this is the entire workflow in one line.

novel_features.name: Doctor with base-URL discovery
novel_features.description: Validate auth, probe both /v1 and /api/v1 mount paths, and report the working integration-API base URL with environment-variable guidance.
novel_features.rationale: Pangolin's spec advertises /v1 but most deployments mount it at /api/v1. Doctor detects the mismatch and tells the user exactly what PANGOLIN_BASE_URL should be.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the user sets up the CLI for the first time, this catches the single most common misconfiguration before any other command fails.

narrative.headline: The first agent-native CLI for Pangolin — every endpoint, plus offline SQLite, cross-org audits, and one-shot config backup and restore the dashboard cannot do.
narrative.value_prop: Pangolin is the open-source, self-hosted alternative to Cloudflare Tunnels. This CLI exposes all 157 integration-API endpoints with --json, --select, --csv, and --dry-run, mirrors the full configuration into a local SQLite store, and adds commands that span orgs — audit, cert-watch, access-graph, backup, restore, expose — that no Pangolin tool offers today.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authentication is a single Bearer integration token. Set PANGOLIN_TOKEN in your environment; the base URL is typically https://<your-dashboard>/api/v1 (note: the OpenAPI spec advertises /v1 but real EE deployments mount the API under /api/v1). Set PANGOLIN_BASE_URL accordingly and run `pangolin-pp-cli doctor` to confirm.

quickstart.comment: Confirm auth and base URL are correct before doing anything else
Mirror every org, site, resource, target, role, and IdP into local SQLite
Cross-org health: stale targets, missing roles, orphaned resources
List certificates expiring in the next 30 days
Snapshot the full configuration for version control or DR

troubleshoots.fix: The OpenAPI spec says /v1 but most EE deployments mount the API at /api/v1. Set PANGOLIN_BASE_URL=https://<your-host>/api/v1 and rerun `pangolin-pp-cli doctor`.
Verify PANGOLIN_TOKEN is set to a valid integration token (created in the dashboard under Integration Tokens). Run `pangolin-pp-cli doctor` to confirm.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from a list command
troubleshoots.fix: Local store may be stale. Run `pangolin-pp-cli sync --full` first, then retry.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI whenever the user mentions Pangolin, exposing a homelab service through a tunnel, managing reverse-proxy resources, or auditing who can reach what on a self-hosted edge proxy. The CLI is the right tool for any Pangolin workflow that involves more than a single resource or a single org, or anything an agent should drive programmatically.

recipes.title: Expose a homelab service in one command
recipes.explanation: Previews the full create-site-resource-target-role chain; drop --dry-run to apply.

recipes.title: Find expiring certs across every org
recipes.explanation: Returns a compact JSON array of certs expiring within 30 days. Pair with --select to shrink the agent's context cost.

recipes.title: Audit access for a specific user
recipes.explanation: Joins users x roles x resources x orgs into a single answer for 'what can this person reach?'.

recipes.title: Disaster-recovery backup
recipes.explanation: Snapshots the entire Pangolin configuration as JSON; commit to git for diffable history.

recipes.title: Search resources by partial name
recipes.explanation: FTS over the local store finds resources without paginating the live API.
